title: The Freehold On The Plain
content:
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  (Air: ï؟½The Little Old Log Cabin in the Lane.ï؟½)


آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m a broken-down old squatter, my cash it is all gone,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Of troubles and bad seasons I complain;
آ آ آ آ My cattle are all mortgaged, of horses I have none,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  The stockyardï؟½s broken down, and the woolshedï؟½s tumbling in;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Iï؟½ve written to the mortgagees in vain;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  My wool it is all damaged and it is not worth a pin,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  And Iï؟½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

آ آ آ آ I commenced life as a squatter some twenty years ago,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ When fortune followed in my train;
آ آ آ آ But I speculated heavy and Iï؟½d have you all to know
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ That Iï؟½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: The stockyardï؟½s broken down, &c.

آ آ آ آ I built myself a mansion, and chose myself a wife;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Of her I have no reason to complain;
آ آ آ آ For I thought I had sufficient to last me all my life,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ But Iï؟½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: The stockyardï؟½s broken down, &c.

آ آ آ آ And now I am compelled to take a droverï؟½s life,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To drive cattle through the sunshine and the rain,
آ آ آ آ And to leave her behind me, my own dear loving wifeï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ We were happy on that freehold on the plain.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: The stockyardï؟½s broken down, &c.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) delivers a powerful performance in "The Freehold On The Plain"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
